claims.claim: The canonical machine-readable Israel Drug Registry is the MoH backend at israeldrugs.health.gov.il/GovServiceList/IDRServer/ (endpoints SearchByName, GetSpecificDrug, SearchGenericByDragRegNum, SearchByPackageBarcode). It returns JSON fields dragHebName, dragEnName, dragRegNum, dosageForm, activeComponents/activeComponentsCompareName, dragRegOwner, prescription (bool), health (bool = in basket), customerPrice/packagesPrices, barcodes, indications, iscanceled.
claims.raw_snippet: The Israel Drug Registry (israeldrugs.health.gov.il) provides a live search over registered medicines; its backend IDRServer API returns drug name, registration number, dosage form, active components, manufacturer, prescription/health-basket flags, and regulated price.

claims.claim: The SearchByName endpoint of the IDRServer API returns drug records as JSON.
claims.raw_snippet: POST israeldrugs.health.gov.il/GovServiceList/IDRServer/SearchByName with a val field returns matching drugs as JSON.

claims.claim: Israel's adverse-event (pharmacovigilance) reporting is the MoH Side Effects Reporting Form (טופס דיווח על תופעות לוואי) run by the MoH Pharmacovigilance and Drug Information Department; 'Yellow Card' is the UK MHRA scheme, not Israel's.
claims.raw_snippet: Israel's Ministry of Health operates a side-effects reporting form (טופס דיווח על תופעות לוואי) for adverse drug events through its Pharmacovigilance and Drug Information Department; the 'Yellow Card' name belongs to the UK MHRA.

claims.claim: The national health basket (sal briut) is updated once a year; a public committee reviews candidates and the new list takes effect in January.
claims.raw_snippet: The health basket (sal ha-briut) is updated annually; the committee's recommendations and the year's additions take effect in January.

claims.claim: The FDA retired the A/B/C/D/X pregnancy letter categories in 2015 (PLLR) in favor of narrative pregnancy and lactation labeling; many Israeli leaflets still reference the legacy letters.
claims.raw_snippet: The Pregnancy and Lactation Labeling Rule (PLLR), effective June 30, 2015, removed the A/B/C/D/X letter categories in favor of narrative labeling.

claims.claim: Approximate, kupah-dependent figures used as ballpark guidance (not fixed regulated values): a generic drug copay is roughly 10-20 NIS / 20 ש"ח per package and a brand-name copay (where a generic exists) roughly 30-50 NIS / 50 ש"ח; generics typically run 40-60% cheaper than the brand. Actual copays are set per kupat cholim, so the skill tells users to confirm with their kupah.
claims.raw_snippet: Copay amounts for subsidized drugs are set by each kupat cholim and vary; generic drugs are substantially cheaper than originator brands. These are approximate ranges, not fixed national prices.

claims.claim: The MoH Pharmaceutical Division publishes the drug registry entry points and safety alerts on gov.il.
claims.raw_snippet: The Pharmaceutical Division of the Israeli Ministry of Health manages drug registration and publishes safety information.

claims.claim: The WHO ATC/DDD index provides the anatomical therapeutic chemical classification used to cross-reference active ingredients.
claims.raw_snippet: The WHO Collaborating Centre for Drug Statistics Methodology maintains the ATC/DDD index.

claims.claim: The gov.il Israeli drug index service page is the primary entry point to the national drug registry.
claims.raw_snippet: The gov.il Israeli drug index service links to the national drug registry search.
